messages.content: okay so... I got the Google offer. $165K base in Mountain View. And Cohere came back with $145K remote. I think I'm gonna take Google but god I'm so anxious about telling Reeves
Congratulations on both offers! That's a great outcome from your interview process. What's making you lean toward Google over the remote option?

honestly the money is part of it but also like... Google feels more legit? I know that's probably shallow but my parents will understand Google in a way they won't get Cohere. Plus Mountain View is expensive but it's where everything happens in ML right now
Those aren't shallow reasons at all - location and company recognition matter for career trajectory. But it sounds like telling Dr. Reeves is weighing on you more than the decision itself?

ugh yes. I keep putting it off and I know that's making it worse. Preethi warned me that he takes it personally when people leave for industry and like... he's been a good advisor, you know? I don't want him to think I'm just using the program as a stepping stone

It's understandable to feel conflicted about disappointing someone who's invested in you. Have you thought about how you want to frame the conversation? Maybe focusing on what you've learned rather than what you're leaving behind?

maybe... I just keep spiraling about it. Like I finally got my thesis draft done and the defense is in May and now I have to have this awkward conversation about how I'm not doing a PhD. I've been losing sleep over it which is stupid because my therapist keeps telling me I can't control other people's reactions

Your therapist is right - you can only control how you approach the conversation, not how he responds. It sounds like you've accomplished a lot with your thesis despite juggling job applications. When are you thinking of having the conversation with Dr. Reeves?

I need to decide on Google by Friday so... probably tomorrow? I accepted mentally already I just need to make it official. And tell Reeves before I sign anything because that feels like the right thing to do even though I'm terrified

That timeline makes sense - giving him a heads up before you officially accept shows respect for your working relationship. You've got this, and remember that choosing industry doesn't diminish the value of what you've accomplished in your MS program.

summary: Kai shares receiving job offers from Google ($165K, Mountain View) and Cohere ($145K, remote), deciding to accept Google. The main anxiety centers on telling advisor Dr. Reeves about leaving academia for industry, with concerns about disappointing him despite completing thesis work.
